INTRO-01-a.mp3: Hey, it's Justin. Welcome to this meditation. I appreciate you taking the time to spend with me and your inner world. Make sure you've got a comfy enough position and environment.
INTRO-01-b.mp3: Hi, it's Justin. Welcome back to another self-regulation practice. Before you start, make sure your environment and position work well enough for you.
PINTRO-ANCHORING-a.mp3: This one is an anchoring practice. We're gonna connect with the present moment and anchor into safety. There, there's nothing to fix. There is nowhere you must get to. We're just focusing on anchoring.
PINTRO-ANCHORING-b.mp3: In this practice, we are going to anchor into safety. We're gonna use your senses to connect to the present moment and hopefully feel a bit more safety in our system.
PINTRO-IMAGERY-a.mp3: This is an imagery and invitation practice. We'll anchor into safety first and then gently bring a non-safety emotion into view. We'll give it an image and invite it to be here with us. We're not fixing it. We're getting familiar with it.
PINTRO-IMAGERY-b.mp3: Up next is imagery and invitation. We anchor in safety, then we'll picture a non-safety emotion and invite it to stay with us as we reconnect, just getting to know it.
PINTRO-OBSTACLES-a.mp3: This is an obstacles practice. We'll anchor into safety, and then I will offer a few statements that tend to bump into our defenses. Whatever surfaces, we will validate it and normalize it and invite it to be with us.
PINTRO-OBSTACLES-b.mp3: In this practice, we work with obstacles. After we anchor in safety, I'll read a few statements and we'll notice what reacts within you. Then we validate, normalize, and invite that reaction to be.
PINTRO-BALANCING-a.mp3: This is a balancing practice. We'll anchor into safety and then invite a little non-safety alongside it and practice holding both at once. Safety stays the priority the whole way through.
PINTRO-BALANCING-b.mp3: Up next is balancing. We anchor in safety, bring in some non-safety, and let the two be present together. We are building the capacity to hold both with safety leading.
PINTRO-PENDULATING-a.mp3: This is a pendulating practice. We'll anchor in safety, then gently move back and forth a little non-safety, back to safety, and again, building flexibility between the two. Safety is always the home base.
PINTRO-PENDULATING-b.mp3: In this one, we pendulate. After anchoring, we'll swing our attention between safety and non-safety back and forth, letting your system learn it can move between them. We always come back to safety.

NUMB-OK-b.mp3: If there's nothing there that you're feeling or it's hard to feel, that is completely okay. The blankness or the numbness is useful, not a failure. Just be with that not muchness for a moment.
